title: crassula helmsii
intro: Crassula helmsii, also known as New Zealand pygmyweed, is a small, succulent aquatic plant native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Crassula helmsii include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
